qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded resource consumption in HTTP file download and processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unbounded HTTP/HuggingFace download buffering in scanner.py _http_get and related scan paths. The advisory targets scan_pytorch magic-number parsing, where pickletools.genops misses a dynamically evaluated MAGIC_NUMBER accepted by PyTorch loading.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-backed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns a Picklescan scan_pytorch bypass in get_magic_number-style magic-number extraction, specifically the mismatch between pickletools.genops seeing INT/LONG opcodes and PyTorch pickle loading evaluating a dynamic MAGIC_NUMBER. The only manifest-backed qualifying finding covers unbounded HTTP download buffering.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-97f8-7cmv-76j2 describes scan_pytorch bypass via dynamic eval MAGIC_NUMBER and pickletools.genops not observing the magic number as INT or LONG.
unbounded_resource_consumption/vulnerability_report.json reports scanner.py _http_get response.read() without size limits and a denial-of-service path through attacker-controlled URLs.
Session text search found no qualifying report evidence for scan_pytorch, get_magic_number, MAGIC_NUMBER, pickletools.genops, or the advisory source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
